summary:
Tuza's conjecture ($	au(G)\leq 2
u(G)$) remains open for general graphs. Since the OPG posting, the conjecture has been confirmed for several important graph classes (treewidth $\leq 6$, threshold graphs, random graphs, planar triangulations with the sharper bound $	au(G)\leq  rac{3}{2}
u(G)$), and Baron–Kahn showed the factor 2 is asymptotically tight for dense graphs. The best known general bound is still Haxell's $(3- rac{3}{23})
u(G)$ from 1999.

since_posted.title: Tuza's Conjecture is Asymptotically Tight for Dense Graphs
since_posted.claim: Constructs arbitrarily large dense graphs where $\tau(G)/(\nu(G))\to 2$, showing the bound in Tuza's conjecture is asymptotically tight and disproving a conjecture by Yuster that it could be improved.

since_posted.title: Multi-transversals for Triangles and the Tuza's Conjecture
since_posted.authors: Parinya Chalermsook, Samir Khuller, Pattara Sukprasert, Sumedha Uniyal
since_posted.claim: Proves that for every $k\geq 2$ there exists a multiset $F\subseteq E(G)$ with $|F|\leq 2k\nu(G)$ such that every triangle meets $F$ at least $k$ times, strengthening Krivelevich's fractional results.

since_posted.title: On Tuza's conjecture for triangulations and graphs with small treewidth
since_posted.authors: Fábio Botler, Cristina G. Fernandes, Juan Gutiérrez
since_posted.claim: Verifies Tuza's conjecture for all graphs with treewidth at most 6, and proves the sharper bound $\tau(G)\leq\frac{3}{2}\nu(G)$ for planar triangulations other than $K_4$.

since_posted.title: Closing the Random Graph Gap in Tuza's Conjecture Through the Online Triangle Packing Process
since_posted.authors: Patrick Bennett, Ryan Cushman, Andrzej Dudek
since_posted.claim: Proves that Tuza's conjecture holds with high probability in the Erdős–Rényi random graph $G(n,m)$ for all ranges of $m$, closing a previously open gap.

since_posted.title: Tuza's Conjecture for Threshold Graphs
since_posted.authors: Marthe Bonamy, Łukasz Bożyk, Andrzej Grzesik, Meike Hatzel, Tomáš Masařík, Jana Novotná, Karolina Okrasa
since_posted.claim: Confirms Tuza's conjecture for threshold graphs (simultaneously split graphs and cographs) and for co-chain graphs satisfying certain divisibility conditions.

search_queries: Tuza conjecture triangle packing edge transversal tau 2nu progress 2013 2024 2025
Tuza's conjecture triangle covering arXiv recent proof partial result
Haxell triangle packing covering improvement bound 2013 2025
notes: The SIAM journal page for Bennett–Cushman–Dudek returned HTTP 403; the DOI 10.1137/20M1351771 is taken from the URL that appeared in search results, while the paper details were confirmed via the arXiv abstract. The 2024 arXiv:2406.06501 paper (Parker) concerns the Aharoni–Zerbib generalization to k-uniform hypergraphs, not Tuza's original conjecture, and was therefore excluded from since_posted. The best general bound remains Haxell's $(3-3/23)\nu(G)$ from 1999; no improvement to the general case was found in post-2013 literature.
